text: Chapter 2 contains no load-bearing prose passages — composed entirely of notated patterns and tab examples over the chord changes of The Days of Wine and Roses. Like Chapter 1, this Study reinforces the book’s consistent template of applying the pattern vocabulary to a major-key standard. The chapter is essentially a worked etude: each pattern from the catalog is placed against specific chord positions in the form, demonstrating how the same melodic cells navigate different harmonic targets.
